purpose: Implement the missing execution architecture for generic goals while reusing existing partial implementation pieces: explicit contracts, LLM-first planning, policy/audit governance, tool intent recovery, improvement loops and artifact-first verification.
goal: Ananta solves broad user goals through the existing hub-worker architecture while planning, execution, tool, security, audit, recovery and verification decisions are explicit, observable and correctable.
program_success_criteria: Hub owns planning, task queue state, routing, policy and orchestration.
Workers execute delegated tasks only and do not create worker-to-worker orchestration loops.
Complex goals default to LLM-first execution with guardrails.
Deterministic execution is explicit strategy or audited final fallback only.
Every goal has or derives a versioned GoalExecutionContract.
Planning defines expected artifacts and verification gates.
Recoverable tool name/schema mistakes are repaired unless policy/security denies them.
Security violations are hard blocked with structured policy evidence.
Tool remaps log original_tool, resolved_tool, intent/risk and reason.
Completion is based on artifacts and verification, not markdown-only output.
Improvement loops are bounded, auditable and hub-controlled.
RAG, role templates, blueprints and team context are visible prompt inputs.
Golden-path tests prove Java backend plus Angular frontend Fibonacci generation in the invocation directory.

milestones.title: Execution Contract Foundation
milestones.summary: Introduce the central contract abstraction and map existing coverage first.

milestones.title: Artifact-Driven Planning
milestones.summary: Make planning output checkable against concrete deliverables.

milestones.title: LLM Proposal Context
milestones.summary: Make role templates, blueprints, team context and RAG first-class prompt inputs.

milestones.title: Tool Intent And Recovery
milestones.summary: Harden existing resolver into canonical intent mapping with policy recheck.

milestones.title: Policy And Audit Backbone
milestones.summary: Unify audit events and policy decisions across execution stages.

milestones.title: Completion And Verification
milestones.summary: Connect existing artifact-first completion to contract-driven expected artifacts.

milestones.title: Golden Path And Operations
milestones.summary: Prove real CLI behavior and expose operator diagnostics.

tasks.title: Classify existing implementation coverage
tasks.summary: Map existing implementation pieces before adding new code.
acceptance_criteria: ToolIntentResolver coverage is mapped to GEC-T013..T016.
TaskCompletionPolicyService coverage is mapped to GEC-T025.
VerificationService coverage is mapped to GEC-T018 and GEC-T026.
Workspace diff/artifact coverage is mapped to GEC-T027.
Task statuses reflect real implementation state.

tasks.title: Define GoalExecutionContract schema
tasks.summary: Create the shared versioned contract abstraction.
acceptance_criteria: GoalExecutionContract JSON-compatible model exists.
Contract contains execution_mode, strategy_mode, expected_artifacts, allowed intents/classes, verification_gates, fallback_policy, audit_requirements and max_improvement_loops.
Contract can be stored in existing goal/task metadata.
Default contract can be generated for legacy goals.
Tests cover defaults, serialization and compatibility.

tasks.title: Attach execution contract at goal creation
tasks.summary: Every new goal receives a contract before planning.
acceptance_criteria: API/CLI goal creation stores GoalExecutionContract metadata.
Contract records LLM-first, deterministic or hybrid strategy.
Contract includes workspace/output constraints.
Legacy clients still work without sending contract fields.
Tests cover explicit, partial and missing contract input.

tasks.title: Propagate contract into materialized tasks
tasks.summary: Worker tasks receive immutable task-scoped contract fragments.
acceptance_criteria: Tasks contain worker_execution_contract or equivalent fragment.
Task records preserve parent contract version/source.
Workers can read fragment without hub-private state.
Existing tasks use compatibility adapter.
Tests cover single-task and multi-task propagation.

tasks.title: Add contract compatibility adapter
tasks.summary: Derive safe contracts from existing records.
acceptance_criteria: Existing records load and execute without migration failures.
Derived contracts include source=compatibility_adapter and version.
Complex software goals default to LLM-first unless policy says otherwise.
Adapter never grants broader tool permissions than legacy behavior.
Regression tests cover representative legacy records.

tasks.title: Extend plan proposal with artifact expectations
tasks.summary: Planning defines expected files, directories, commands, tests and evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Planning output supports expected_artifacts.
Java+Angular goals expect source structures, not markdown-only notes.
Invalid artifact-producing plans are rejected or repaired.
Expected artifacts propagate into task contracts.
Tests cover valid, missing and malformed expectations.

tasks.title: Add software project plan blueprint
tasks.summary: Reusable blueprint guidance for generated software projects.
acceptance_criteria: software_project blueprint has backend/frontend/integration/verification phases.
Blueprint expresses Java backend and Angular frontend expectations.
Blueprint is available to LLM planning prompts and validation.
Blueprint does not create worker-to-worker orchestration.
Tests verify blueprint selection.

tasks.title: Add plan repair for missing deliverables
tasks.summary: Repair invalid plans before execution.
acceptance_criteria: Plan validation returns structured deliverable errors.
LLM repair receives validation errors and contract.
Repaired plans are revalidated.
Repeated repair failure becomes needs_review.
Tests cover successful repair and bounded failure.

tasks.title: Persist plan-to-artifact traceability
tasks.summary: Trace plan item to task to expected/produced artifacts to verification.
acceptance_criteria: Expected artifacts link to plan item and task.
Produced artifacts match expectations.
Verification results reference expected artifacts.
Trace is queryable from task/goal status.
Tests cover multi-task trace lookup.

tasks.title: Create prompt context bundle model
tasks.summary: One auditable bundle for role, task, contract, blueprint, RAG and policy context.
acceptance_criteria: PromptContextBundle model exists.
Bundle records context classes and source identifiers.
LLM strategies consume it without retrieval internals.
Bundle is auditable without storing sensitive raw content by default.
Tests cover bundle assembly with and without RAG.

tasks.title: Feed RAG and instruction layers into LLM strategies
tasks.summary: Prompt strategies receive filtered bundle context directly.
acceptance_criteria: Tool-calling and JSON-schema strategies include bundle data.
Prompt includes contract summary, artifact expectations, allowed intents and gates.
Prompt includes filtered RAG/instruction context with labels.
Audit records included context classes.
Tests verify required fields and blocked context exclusion.

tasks.title: Add prompt budget and redaction policy checks
tasks.summary: Prompt enrichment is bounded, redacted and explainable.
acceptance_criteria: Context budget is enforced.
Sensitive classes are filtered or redacted before LLM calls.
Audit states included/excluded/redacted classes.
Policy violations produce needs_review or blocked.
Tests cover over-budget, redacted and denied context.

tasks.title: Add role and team template selection audit
tasks.summary: Role, team and blueprint selections are observable.
acceptance_criteria: Selections are recorded with reason.
Selection metadata links to LLM proposal audit.
Operators can inspect metadata from history/status.
Prompt hashes or bundle IDs allow correlation.
Tests cover default and explicit modes.

tasks.title: Define canonical tool intent taxonomy
tasks.summary: Stable intent classes replace fragile raw tool names.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical tool intent taxonomy exists in code/docs.
Each intent declares args, risk class and allowed remap targets.
Contract can restrict allowed intents.
Unknown raw names are evaluated against taxonomy before failure.
Tests cover known, alias and unknown lookup.

tasks.title: Harden ToolIntentResolver remapping
tasks.summary: Existing resolver already remaps conservatively; harden it into contract-aware resolution.
acceptance_criteria: Unknown safe names are not failed immediately when args map clearly.
Alias mapping supports shell and file-write variants.
Argument remapping resolves path/content to file_write and path-only to file_read.
Command payloads remain conservative and need shell approval.
Every remap includes original_tool, resolved_tool, intent, risk and reason.

tasks.title: Integrate resolver with execution policy
tasks.summary: Remapping must not bypass policy.
acceptance_criteria: Resolved tools are checked against task-scoped allowed intents/classes.
Post-remap policy denial prevents execution.
Security denial differs from recoverable schema/name failure.
Audit includes pre-resolution tool, resolved tool, policy decision and reason.
Tests prove remapped dangerous command cannot bypass policy.

tasks.title: Add resolver regression tests
tasks.summary: Protect alias/remap/recoverable/block behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover alias remap to shell intent where allowed.
Tests cover argument remap to file_write.
Tests cover ambiguous unknown tools returning recoverable needs_review/todo.
Tests cover hard security blocks.
Tests assert original_tool, resolved_tool, intent/risk and reason.

tasks.title: Define improvement loop state machine
tasks.summary: LLM repair loop is explicit, bounded and hub-controlled.
acceptance_criteria: State machine is documented.
Hub owns counters, transitions and terminal decisions.
Workers return outcomes/hints but dispatch no new worker tasks.
Loop respects max_improvement_loops.
Tests cover success, repair, needs_review and blocked paths.

tasks.title: Implement verification critique generation
tasks.summary: VerificationService already classifies failures; extend into critique payloads.
acceptance_criteria: Failed verification creates critique payload.
Critique references expected artifacts and mismatches.
Critique includes logs/evidence refs after redaction.
Critique is stored in history/audit trace.
Tests cover missing files and failed commands.

tasks.title: Add LLM repair proposal strategy
tasks.summary: LLM proposes repairs for failed outcomes within contract limits.
acceptance_criteria: Repair strategy accepts critique, contract and prompt bundle.
Strategy returns normalized tool proposals or plan adjustments.
Repair proposals are policy-checked before execution.
Repeated failures stop at max_improvement_loops and enter needs_review.
Tests use fake provider, not live LLM.

tasks.title: Add loop telemetry and operator status
tasks.summary: Expose current loop stage and next action safely.
acceptance_criteria: Task status includes loop stage and attempt count.
Status includes safe last error and next action.
Status distinguishes blocked, needs_review, repairing and deterministic_fallback.
Sensitive details are redacted.
Tests cover status read model.

tasks.title: Define unified execution audit event schema
tasks.summary: One coherent audit vocabulary for execution decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Unified audit schema is documented and represented in code.
Events include trace_id, goal_id, task_id, actor_role, operation_type, policy_version, outcome and timestamp.
Tool remap events include original_tool, resolved_tool and reason.
Prompt events include context metadata and redaction summary.
Tests validate representative event types.

tasks.title: Emit audit events across default path
tasks.summary: Default execution is reconstructable from audit events.
acceptance_criteria: Default-path run emits audit events for each major stage.
Events correlate by trace_id from goal to completion.
Security blocks and recoverable errors are distinguishable.
Fallback decisions record reason and policy source.
Smoke test proves event coverage.

tasks.title: Add hard security block semantics
tasks.summary: Security violations differ from recoverable tool/schema issues.
acceptance_criteria: Outcomes distinguish security_denied, policy_denied, recoverable_invalid_tool, recoverable_schema_error, needs_review and failed.
Hard security blocks do not auto-repair unless safe re-planning is explicitly allowed.
Recoverable errors can enter repair or needs_review.
Status/audit expose normalized outcome class.
Tests cover all outcome classes.

tasks.title: Add audit redaction and retention rules for prompts
tasks.summary: Audit stays useful without storing sensitive prompt data blindly.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt audit policy defines default/debug/restricted retention modes.
Secrets and credentials are redacted by default.
Audit uses hashes, context classes and source refs.
Policy is configurable without code changes.
Tests cover sensitive fields.

tasks.title: Make artifact-first completion authoritative
tasks.summary: Existing TaskCompletionPolicyService is artifact-first; bind it to contract expected_artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Artifact-producing tasks require expected artifact checks before completion.
Missing required files/dirs prevents completion.
Markdown-only output does not satisfy software project completion unless expected.
Completion results include passed, failed and skipped gates.
Tests cover success and missing-artifact failure.

tasks.title: Implement software project verification gates
tasks.summary: Existing verification checks artifact presence; extend to Java/Angular gates.
acceptance_criteria: Verification checks Java backend structure.
Verification checks Angular frontend structure.
Verification checks Fibonacci calculation/endpoint evidence.
Verification can run configured build/test commands when allowed.
Verification produces structured evidence for critique/audit.

tasks.title: Add artifact snapshot and diff evidence
tasks.summary: Execution already snapshots workspaces and records FileChangeSet; standardize evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Execution captures pre/post manifests.
Manifests include path, type, size and hash where practical.
Produced evidence links to expected artifacts.
Evidence is available to critique generation.
Tests cover file creation and modification.

tasks.title: Add completion regression tests
tasks.summary: Existing artifact-first tests exist; extend them against contract and software gates.
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail on markdown-only software output.
Tests pass when backend/frontend artifacts exist.
Tests cover failed verification entering repair/needs_review.
Tests cover blocked dependency command behavior.
Tests run locally without live Ollama.

tasks.title: Create CLI golden-path e2e for Java and Angular Fibonacci project
tasks.summary: Existing Fibonacci E2E is Python/Flask fake-provider; extend to real CLI Java+Angular.
acceptance_criteria: E2E creates fresh temp working directory.
CLI is invoked from inside that directory.
Generated files are created inside directory or declared output child.
Project contains Java backend Fibonacci calculation.
Project contains Angular frontend input/display.
E2E verifies completion status and artifacts.

tasks.title: Add LLM and Ollama activity observability
tasks.summary: Some trace/flow metrics exist; expose explicit LLM provider/model diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: Status/diagnostics show whether LLM calls were attempted.
Diagnostics include provider, model, timestamp and outcome.
Diagnostics distinguish planner from worker LLM calls.
Failures include unavailable_provider, timeout or policy_denied categories.
Tests cover diagnostic formatting.

tasks.title: Document deterministic fallback policy
tasks.summary: Deterministic behavior is explicit or audited fallback only.
acceptance_criteria: Docs state deterministic fallback rules per strategy mode.
Default complex-goal mode is LLM-first with guardrails.
Fallback requires explicit policy reason and audit event.
Task status shows fallback usage.
Tests cover LLM unavailable with fallback allowed/denied.

tasks.title: Refactor oversized default execution path seams
tasks.summary: Reduce SRP pressure by extracting contract, audit, transition and completion seams.
acceptance_criteria: At least one focused service/collaborator is extracted.
Existing autopilot behavior remains compatible.
Contract lookup, audit or completion no longer requires editing central dispatch directly.
Existing tests pass after refactor.
Developer note documents the seam.

execution_stage_summary.stage_0_coverage: First classify existing partial implementation so the track reuses current services instead of rebuilding them.
execution_stage_summary.stage_1_contract: Create and attach a versioned GoalExecutionContract using existing metadata fields first.
execution_stage_summary.stage_2_planning: Require LLM planning to produce concrete artifact expectations and repair invalid plans before execution.
execution_stage_summary.stage_3_context: Feed role templates, blueprints, team context and filtered RAG through one auditable prompt bundle.
execution_stage_summary.stage_4_tools: Resolve tool intent through taxonomy/alias/argument mapping, then re-run policy against normalized intents.
execution_stage_summary.stage_5_loop: Run bounded hub-controlled improvement loops over propose, execute, verify, critique and repair.
execution_stage_summary.stage_6_audit: Emit unified audit events for security-relevant decisions and recovery/fallback transitions.
execution_stage_summary.stage_7_completion: Use contract-driven artifact-first verification as authority for artifact-producing goals.
execution_stage_summary.stage_8_e2e: Prove real CLI behavior by generating/verifying Java backend plus Angular Fibonacci frontend.

summary_notes: Code review showed several requested building blocks already exist partially; this file now marks those as partial instead of todo.
The most important missing piece remains the GoalExecutionContract because it binds planning, execution, policy, verification and fallback.
Do not replace existing ProposeStrategyOrchestrator, ToolIntentResolver, VerificationService or TaskCompletionPolicyService; extend them behind cleaner contract/policy/audit seams.
Default complex-goal path should remain LLM-first with guardrails; deterministic fallback must be explicit and audited.
Tool remapping must be permissive for recoverable name/schema mistakes but strict after policy/security recheck.
Completion must be artifact-driven; successful tool exit or markdown note is not enough for generated software projects.
The hub-worker architecture is preserved: hub owns orchestration, workers execute delegated work only.

end_summaries.title: Current Coverage Summary
items: GoalExecutionContract does not exist yet.
GoalService already has workflow/mode/policy defaults that can feed the contract adapter.
ProposeStrategyOrchestrator and ProposeContext already exist and should receive PromptContextBundle rather than being replaced.
ToolIntentResolver already performs conservative remapping and should be hardened into taxonomy/intent/policy-aware resolution.
TaskCompletionPolicyService, VerificationService and workspace diff/artifact collection already provide partial artifact-first completion coverage.

end_summaries.title: Target Architecture Summary
items: User goals enter the hub and receive a versioned GoalExecutionContract.
Hub uses the contract to guide LLM planning, materialization, allowed intents, verification gates and fallback rules.
Workers receive immutable task-scoped contract fragments and execute only delegated work.
Every LLM, tool, policy, repair, verification and fallback decision is auditable.
Completion is decided through expected artifacts and verification evidence.

end_summaries.title: Implementation Order Summary
items: Start with coverage classification and contract compatibility adapter.
Attach contract to goals and propagate fragments into tasks.
Connect plan artifact expectations to task contracts.
Harden ToolIntentResolver and policy recheck before execution.
Connect artifact-first completion to contract expected_artifacts.
Finish with Java+Angular CLI golden path and LLM diagnostics.

end_summaries.title: Definition Of Done Summary
items: CLI can create Java backend plus Angular Fibonacci frontend through LLM-first default path.
Generated files appear in invocation directory or declared output child.
Run cannot complete with only placeholder markdown.
Audit/status output explains planning, tool resolution, execution, verification, repair and completion decisions.
security_denied, policy_denied and recoverable tool/schema failures are distinct.

execution_order_sprints.title: Contract Core Closure
execution_order_sprints.goal: Close the architecture bottleneck around contract authority and unblock downstream tracks.
exit_criteria: GoalExecutionContract schema and defaults are stable and versioned.
New and legacy goals both receive valid contracts.

execution_order_sprints.title: Loop And Audit Backbone
execution_order_sprints.goal: Make execution transitions and evidence chain explicit before deep verification work.
exit_criteria: Hub-controlled loop states are active and bounded.
Unified audit event schema is emitted across default path transitions.
Hard security blocks and recoverable errors are clearly separated in status and audit.

execution_order_sprints.title: Artifact Completion Hardening
execution_order_sprints.goal: Turn partial completion and verification tasks into production-grade gates.
exit_criteria: Artifact-first completion is authoritative for artifact-producing goals.
Software verification gates for Java plus Angular outputs are active.
Critique payload and regression tests cover both pass and fail outcomes.

execution_order_sprints.title: Golden Path Reliability
execution_order_sprints.goal: Prove real-world CLI behavior and close observability gaps for LLM usage.
exit_criteria: Golden-path CLI e2e run produces expected Java and Angular artifacts.
LLM or Ollama usage diagnostics are visible and actionable.
Deterministic fallback usage is explicit and audited.

definition_of_done: Resolver handles alias and argument-based remap paths for shell and file intents.
Ambiguous or invalid schema/name cases return recoverable outcomes instead of immediate hard fail.
Every remap emits structured fields original_tool, resolved_tool and reason.
Unit tests cover remap success, ambiguity and policy rejection boundaries.
Verification failures generate structured critique payloads with expectation mismatch evidence.
Critique payload is persisted and linked to task history and trace IDs.
Critique payload is consumable by repair strategy without leaking restricted context.
Tests cover missing artifacts and failed command verification cases.
Artifact-first completion is integrated into default completion authority.
Artifact-producing goals cannot complete on markdown-only output unless explicitly expected.
Completion response reports gate-level pass/fail outcomes.
Regression tests verify both successful and blocked completion decisions.
Java backend and Angular frontend verification gates are implemented and configurable.
Verification checks detect core Fibonacci functionality evidence.
Blocked command environments are reported as blocked or needs_review with explicit reason.
Verification outputs are structured for audit and repair consumption.
Pre/post execution artifact manifests are captured for artifact-producing tasks.
Manifest entries include path, type and stable integrity metadata.
Manifest evidence is linked to expected artifacts and verification outcomes.
Tests cover create, modify and no-change scenarios.
Completion regression suite fails for placeholder-only outputs.
Completion regression suite passes for valid Java and Angular artifact trees.
Suite covers repair or needs_review transitions after verification failure.
Suite runs locally without mandatory live Ollama dependency.
CLI e2e creates a temporary invocation directory and executes from within it.
Generated project contains expected Java backend and Angular frontend artifact sets.
Goal/task status transitions reach verified completion state.
E2E output includes evidence references suitable for audit review.
Diagnostics expose planner and worker LLM call attempts separately.
Provider, model, timestamp and failure category are visible in status or diagnostics.
Default reporting remains metadata-only without raw prompt content leakage.
Tests cover normal and LLM-unavailable diagnostic output.

summaries.why_this_track_exists: Current code has planning strategies, LLM proposal strategies, RAG filtering, tool intent resolution and artifact completion pieces, but they are not bound by one explicit execution contract.
summaries.first_implementation_slice: Run GEC-T000, then implement GEC-T001/GEC-T004/GEC-T002/GEC-T003/GEC-T005, then GEC-T013..GEC-T015, then GEC-T025..GEC-T029.
summaries.main_risks: Broad tick-engine edits, hidden policy bypass through remapped tools, accidental worker-side orchestration and tests that pass without proving real artifact generation.
summaries.definition_of_done_for_track: Complete when CLI reliably creates Java+Angular Fibonacci through LLM-first default path with artifact-based completion and reconstructable audit evidence.
